channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pauline Hanson and Angus Taylor clash over One Nation policies and election strategies
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has intensified criticism of the minor party One Nation, calling their policies 'incoherent' and warning they could exacerbate Australia's economic crisis. In response, ...

item.title: BLACKPINK's Lisa Spotted at Family Dinner with Mystery Man, Sparks Speculation
item.description: BLACKPINK member Lisa's personal life has once again become a focal point of public speculation following a viral video clip allegedly capturing her at a family dinner with her parents and an unidenti...

item.title: Bhatti Vikramarka Criticizes BRS Leaders for Misleading Claims on Government Welfare Schemes
item.description: Deputy Chief Minister Mallu Bhatti Vikramarka has accused Bharat Rashtra Samiti (BRS) leaders of attempting to mislead the public about the welfare schemes implemented by the Congress government in Te...

item.title: Critics argue NATO's support for Ukraine prolongs conflict and risks European stability
item.description: The original text presents a strongly opinionated critique of NATO's involvement in the俄乌战争 (Ukraine-Russia war), arguing that continued Western military support for Ukraine is exacerbating geopolitic...

item.title: Trump's New Air Force One Lacks Key Defensive Systems, Report Warns
item.description: President Donald Trump's newly acquired Qatari jet, used for recent international travel, has raised security concerns due to its lack of advanced defensive systems compared to older Air Force One mod...

item.title: Trump Avoids Signing Bipartisan Housing Bill Amid National Crisis
item.description: President Donald Trump faces mounting pressure as a bipartisan housing bill, the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, approaches its 10-day deadline for presidential action. The legislation, designed to ...

item.title: Celebrity Drama: Joan Mwaniki and Nicah The Queen Clash Over Dr Ofweneke
item.description: Joan Mwaniki, a prominent Kenyan media personality, has publicly criticized singer Nicah The Queen for unnecessarily inserting herself into a controversy involving comedian Dr Ofweneke. In a video sha...

item.title: The Alibi Episode 83: Stella and Vincent Navigate New Challenges Together
item.description: The Alibi, a popular Philippine television series, continues to captivate audiences with its latest episode, Episode 83. This installment features the dynamic duo of Stella Morales and Vincent Cabrera...

item.title: Zulu royal family to address viral video controversy involving King Misuzulu
item.description: The royal house of Zulu King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has announced plans to convene internally to address viral videos circulating on social media. The footage, which has sparked widespread debate, show...

item.title: Lanao del Sur governor offers reward for information on ambusher attack on BFP personnel
item.description: The governor of Lanao del Sur, Adiong, has offered a cash incentive for any information leading to the arrest of gunmen who ambushed Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) personnel in Calanogas. The ambush ...

item.title: June Jobs Report Shows 18,000 New Positions and 6.5% Unemployment Rate
item.description: Statistics Canada will release June employment data showing a decline in the unemployment rate to 6.5%, with employers adding 18,000 jobs. The report, published on July 10, 2026, indicates a modest im...

item.title: Top Universities Admitting Most Students via 2026 KUCCPS Placement
item.description: The 2026 KUCCPS placement exercise has revealed which universities are set to welcome the highest number of first-year students. Maseno University leads with 9,196 admissions, followed by Moi Universi...

item.title: China successfully tests sea-based rocket booster recovery system
item.description: China has successfully tested a new rocket booster recovery system using a net attached to a sea platform, marking a significant step toward reusable rocket technology. The test, conducted by state me...

item.title: Federal High Court Postpones Judgment in Malami Properties Confiscation Case
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja adjourned the judgment in the EFCC's forfeiture case against 57 properties linked to former Minister of Justice, Abubakar Malami, until July 15. The case, originally sc...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay Blames Police for Karur Stampede Lapses, Announces Memorial Plans
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin criticized the police for failing to alert him about the surging crowd at his rally in Karur, which led to a stampede on September 27 that claimed 41 lives. Durin...

item.title: Essential Pieces for a Summer Capsule Wardrobe
item.description: This article explores the concept of a summer capsule wardrobe, emphasizing the importance of curating a small, versatile collection of clothing that meets all seasonal needs. It highlights how a caps...

item.title: Celebrating Yolngu Culture Through Ramingining's First Fashion Week
item.description: A remote arts centre in Ramingining, East Arnhem Land, hosted its inaugural fashion week, spotlighting local Aboriginal artists, models, and community members. The event served as a platform to celebr...

item.title: High-Profile Cases Handled by Ex-CCT Chairman Danladi Umar
item.description: The article discusses the legal cases involving former Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT) Chairman Danladi Umar, who was remanded in custody after being charged with corruption and abuse of office. Umar, ...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Condemns Criticism of His 'Lavish Lifestyle'
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel responded vehemently to a critical op-ed by MS NOW’s Hayes Brown, who accused him of indulging in a 'lavish lifestyle' funded by taxpayers. The critique highlighted Patel's all...

item.title: Sindh Government Orders Karachi Private School to Reverse Unlawful Fee Increases
item.description: The Sindh government has directed a private school in Karachi to immediately reverse unlawful fee hikes imposed during summer holidays. In a formal letter dated July 10, the Directorate of Inspection ...
